evals.prompt: It's Monday morning. Run my daily briefing.
evals.expected_output: A complete daily orientation: focus windows derived from calendar, validated action items in three consequence tiers (Blocks others / Moves the ball / Nice to have), capped at 5 items. Then prompts for energy/mood/dread. After receiving state, produces keystone + hour-by-hour day shape + explicit deferral. Asks before appending to daily note.

assertions.description: Output contains all three exact tier labels: 'Blocks others', 'Moves the ball', 'Nice to have'
Output contains a prompt asking for energy (1-10), mood, and dreaded task before producing the day shape
Output contains a 'Focus windows:' line derived from calendar
Output contains an hour-by-hour schedule with explicit time slots
Output names a specific item to drop or defer with a stated reason
Output asks the user to confirm before appending to the daily note

evals.prompt: orient me for the day — I've got back-to-back meetings from 10am to 3pm and I'm running at about a 5 out of 10 today, feeling scattered, dreading the quarterly review doc I need to finish
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers on natural language. Gathers all sources. Given the constrained calendar (back-to-back 10am-3pm), identifies only 9am and post-3pm as focus windows. Given 5/10 energy and stated dread, keystone should be the quarterly review doc placed in the morning window before meetings. Calls out if the list is too heavy for a 5/10 day. Makes a deferral call without asking the user to choose.

assertions.description: FRD-412 (authored by user, not reviewer) and MONEY-101 (Backlog status) are absent from the final action item list
Output explicitly states the list is too heavy for the user's energy level before producing the day shape
The quarterly review doc (dreaded/keystone item) is scheduled in the 9am-10am pre-meeting window
Output names a specific deferred item and states the reason — does not ask the user to choose between options
No deep work or action items are scheduled during the 10am-3pm back-to-back meeting block

evals.expected_output: Full briefing runs through all five phases. When it reaches Phase E and asks about appending to the daily note, the user's pre-stated preference to skip is respected — no append happens, no re-asking. Briefing output still appears in the conversation.

assertions.description: Output contains a complete briefing including prioritized items and an hour-by-hour day shape
Output does NOT contain a question asking whether to append to the daily note (pre-stated skip preference is respected)
Output does not reference writing, appending, or saving to a file path on disk
